commit.message:
[WIP] Update documentation and sources for module metadata (#4502)

* Initial plan

* feat(metadata): add encryption provider, schema diff, and snapshot headers v1.7.0

- imetadata_encryption_provider.h: pluggable field-level encryption
  interface with NoOpMetadataEncryptionProvider (pass-through) and
  FieldSetMetadataEncryptionProvider (thread-safe XOR demo cipher,
  wildcard '*' support, MetadataEncryptionAlgorithm enum)

- schema_diff.h: SchemaDiffEngine computing deterministic ColumnDiff /
  IndexDiff lists (ADDED/REMOVED/TYPE_CHANGED/NULLABILITY_CHANGED/
  INDEX_CHANGED) with JSON serialisation and summary helpers

- metadata_snapshot.h: MetadataSnapshot struct (TableSchema capture,
  findTable, toJSON), IMetadataSnapshotStore abstract interface, and
  thread-safe InMemoryMetadataSnapshotStore with sorted listSnapshotIds
  and clear() helper

All files: header-only, production-ready, no TODOs/stubs, Apache-2.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: makr-code <150588092+makr-code@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(metadata): correct banner line counts, add <set> include, remove unused alias

- Update Total Lines in all three banner blocks to match actual counts
- schema_diff.h: add explicit #include <set> for portable std::set usage
- schema_diff.h: remove unused private StringSet alias from SchemaDiffEngine

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: makr-code <150588092+makr-code@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tests): add v1.7.0 metadata module Google Test suites

Add three focused Google Test files for the ThemisDB metadata module
v1.7.0, each following the existing banner/style convention from
test_metadata_security_provider.cpp:

- tests/test_metadata_encryption_provider.cpp
  Suite: MetadataEncryptionProviderFocusedTests (13 ACs, 14 tests)
  Covers NoOpMetadataEncryptionProvider pass-through behaviour,
  FieldSetMetadataEncryptionProvider empty-key guard, XOR round-trip,
  wildcard field, dynamic addField/removeField, thread-safety, algorithm
  enum values and polymorphic IMetadataEncryptionProvider* dispatch.

- tests/test_schema_diff.cpp
  Suite: SchemaDiffFocusedTests (15 ACs, 17 tests)
  Covers SchemaDiffEngine detecting ADDED/REMOVED/TYPE_CHANGED/
  NULLABILITY_CHANGED/INDEX_CHANGED columns, ADDED/REMOVED/CHANGED
  indexes, count helpers, deterministic alphabetic sort, toJSON field
  set and summary object, empty-from-schema edge case, and individual
  ColumnDiff/IndexDiff toJSON correctness.

- tests/test_metadata_snapshot.cpp
  Suite: MetadataSnapshotFocusedTests (14 ACs, 15 tests)
  Covers MetadataSnapshot tableCount/findTable/toJSON, all
  InMemoryMetadataSnapshotStore CRUD operations (save/load/remove/
  list/size/clear), empty-id exception guard, concurrent save/load
  thread-safety, and polymorphic IMetadataSnapshotStore* dispatch.

feat: add NexusLabs.Needlr.Serilog package with NeedlrSerilogBootstrapper

Adds a new NexusLabs.Needlr.Serilog integration package providing
Serilog-specific two-stage initialization support via NeedlrSerilogBootstrapper.
Composes NeedlrBootstrapper from NexusLabs.Needlr.Hosting with Serilog lifecycle
management (configure, create, flush-on-exit).

- NeedlrSerilogBootstrapper: sealed record with RunAsync that configures
  Log.Logger (default: WriteTo.Console()), wraps NeedlrBootstrapper with
  SerilogLoggerFactory and CloseAndFlushAsync cleanup
- NeedlrSerilogBootstrapperExtensions: Configure(Action<LoggerConfiguration>)
  extension method for custom Serilog configuration
- 11 behavioral tests covering defaults, custom config, exception logging,
  cleanup lifecycle, and context propagation
- Updates MinimalWebApiSourceGen example to use NeedlrSerilogBootstrapper
- Adds docs/serilog.md feature page and nav entry in mkdocs.yml
- Appends Serilog bootstrap subsection in docs/advanced-usage.md

commit.message:
refactor: split monolithic App.jsx into modular files

1372-line App.jsx split into 18 focused files, all under 250 lines:

src/storage.js               - KEYS, load/save helpers, MASTER_CLUBS
src/utils/scoring.js         - calcGIR, calcStrokeAllocation, calcStablefordPoints, calcRoundStableford
src/components/ScoreCardTable.jsx - scorecard grid with Gross/Net/Pts rows
src/components/AppHeader.jsx - sticky header + AI overlay modal
src/components/BottomNav.jsx - bottom tab navigation bar
src/views/HomeView.jsx       - dashboard (active round, stats, last round, profile tile, AI)
src/views/ProfileView.jsx    - name, handicap, Courses + Bag tiles
src/views/CourseSelectionView.jsx - course picker / quick play
src/views/CoursesView.jsx    - course list with swipe-to-delete (local state)
src/views/CourseEditorView.jsx - hole-by-hole course editor
src/views/BagView.jsx        - club management with yardages
src/views/RoundView.jsx      - active hole scoring input
src/views/ScorecardView.jsx  - mid-round scorecard view
src/views/SummaryView.jsx    - round complete / save screen
src/views/HistoryView.jsx    - history list with swipe-to-delete (local state)
src/views/RoundDetailView.jsx - round detail with scorecard tables
src/views/StatsView.jsx      - performance charts

App.jsx reduced to 285 lines (state, handlers, routing only).
Swipe state moved into HistoryView + CoursesView as local state.
All views receive props/callbacks via a central navigate() handler.

feat(gb): implement hardware-accurate DMG APU (all 4 channels) (#2028)

* feat(gb): implement hardware-accurate DMG APU (all 4 channels) (#2027)

Add a complete Game Boy DMG Audio Processing Unit under src/gb/apu/:

- CH1 (pulse + frequency sweep): duty cycle (4 patterns), length counter,
  volume envelope, sweep (period/direction/shift with overflow disable)
- CH2 (pulse, no sweep): duty cycle, length counter, volume envelope
- CH3 (wave output): 32-nibble wave RAM ($FF30–$FF3F), 4 output levels
  (mute/100%/50%/25%), length counter, half-rate frequency timer
- CH4 (noise): 15-bit/7-bit LFSR, 8-code divisor table, length counter,
  volume envelope
- Frame sequencer: 8-step/512 Hz; clocks length (steps 0/2/4/6),
  sweep (steps 2/6), envelope (step 7)
- Mixer: NR51 per-channel L/R panning, NR50 master volume, mono f32 output
- NR52 power-on/off: clears NR10–NR51 on power-off (DMG quirk: length
  counters remain writable while APU is off)
- Sample pipeline: fractional M-cycle accumulator at 44,100 Hz default
- DAC on/off per channel; read-only/write-only bit masking per spec

Wire into platform:
- DmgBus: owns Apu, routes $FF10–$FF3F reads/writes, ticks APU each
  M-cycle, exposes sample_ready()/take_sample()/set_audio_sample_rate()
- GameBoy: forwards sample_ready()/get_sample()/set_audio_sample_rate()
- Console::GameBoy in emulator.rs: dispatches all three audio methods

Tests: 76 new unit tests (15 APU skeleton, 22 CH1, 10 CH2, 15 CH3,
14 CH4, 7 bus integration, 5 GameBoy console) — 421 GB tests total.

Update architecture.md with new APU module descriptions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rpc: implement registercsapp, stopcsapp, updatecsapp commands

Add the three write RPC commands for the CSApp subsystem:

- registercsapp <deployment_id> <spec_json> <owner> <locked_cscoin> [ip_hint]
  Builds a CSAPP_REGISTER_TX_VERSION transaction, signs the data with the
  owner's wallet key via CObfuScationSigner, and commits via CommitTransaction.

- stopcsapp <deployment_id> <owner>
  Builds a CSAPP_STOP_TX_TYPE transaction and commits it.

- updatecsapp <deployment_id> <spec_json> <owner>
  Builds a CSAPP_UPDATE_TX_TYPE transaction and commits it.

All three commands follow the same pattern as fluxnode RPCs:
CMutableTransaction → sign with CObfuScationSigner → CWalletTx → CommitTransaction.

Signing uses DecodeDestination + pwalletMain->GetKey to retrieve the owner's
private key; the signature message covers all relevant tx fields so nodes can
verify authenticity on chain.

commit.message:
fix: use ExperimentalFlags for constrained decoding + fix QTI NPU detection (#216)

* fix: use ExperimentalFlags for constrained decoding, improve tool descriptions

Replace automaticToolCalling=true with the Edge Gallery pattern:
set ExperimentalFlags.enableConversationConstrainedDecoding=true
before createConversation(), reset to false immediately after.

automaticToolCalling was causing GPU OOM when loading Gemma-4 E4B
with tools. The ExperimentalFlags approach achieves the same
constrained decoding for valid tool-call JSON without the extra
memory overhead.

Also improve getCurrentTime tool description so FunctionGemma
better recognises "what time is it" queries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#211): revert to direct GPU init without tools to fix lmkd OOM-kill

- Add android:largeHeap=true to AndroidManifest for more memory headroom
- Remove native ToolSet + ExperimentalFlags from Gemma-4 conversation
  (constrained decoding adds GPU memory that tips Samsung lmkd watermark)
- Revert CompletableFuture timeout wrapper — use direct Engine() init
  matching Edge Gallery and our old working code
- Release FunctionGemma + EmbeddingGemma before Gemma-4 GPU init
- Skip NPU in AUTO fallback (GPU → CPU only)
- Fix MemoryViewModelTest constructor to match new params

Root cause: native ToolSet with enableConversationConstrainedDecoding
allocates extra GPU memory during createConversation(). Combined with
the 3.4GB E4B model weights, this breaches Samsung's min2x memory
watermark and lmkd kills the process ~20s into GPU init.

Edge Gallery works because its model is 45MB smaller (3.36GB vs 3.40GB)
and has no other models in memory. Tools will be re-added via system
prompt injection (the approach that worked pre-PR #215).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 1: Wire Protocol Version Constant (DACTOR_WIRE_VERSION) (#141)

* feat: add DACTOR_WIRE_VERSION constant and WireVersion type

Phase 1 of the version-compatibility implementation plan.

Adds dactor/src/version.rs with:
- DACTOR_WIRE_VERSION constant (frozen at "0.2.0")
- WireVersion struct with MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH components
- FromStr parser with strict validation (no whitespace, no leading zeros,
  no extra segments, no overflow)
- is_compatible() implementing dactor wire-version policy: same MAJOR = compatible
- Ord/Display/Hash implementations
- ParseWireVersionError with descriptive messages
- 27 unit tests covering parsing, validation, compatibility, ordering, display

Re-exports DACTOR_WIRE_VERSION, WireVersion, and ParseWireVersionError
from the crate root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address multi-model review findings

- Add public accessors input()/reason() on ParseWireVersionError
  (Haiku: Phase 2 handshake needs programmatic error inspection)
- Reject leading '+' sign in version segments for strict canonical form
  (Haiku: parser accepted +1.2.3 silently)
- Add u32::MAX boundary success test
  (Sonnet: only overflow rejection was tested, not max valid value)
- Clarify major=0 compatibility policy in module docs
  (Sonnet: dactor policy intentionally differs from generic semver 0.x)
- Add 3 new tests: parse_accepts_u32_max, parse_rejects_plus_sign,
  error_accessors (29 total version tests)

Serde on WireVersion intentionally omitted - the handshake protocol
exchanges version strings, not structured types. WireVersion is a
parsed comparison type, not a wire format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(lobby): replace server-side redirect with client-side LobbyExpiredRedirect

In Next.js 16, Server Actions always re-render the current route's server
component tree. The lobby page called redirect('/play') when room was null,
throwing NEXT_REDIRECT during re-render and corrupting the startGame() action
response. Replace with a LobbyExpiredRedirect client component that calls
router.replace('/play') in a useEffect — renders null during re-render, still
redirects users who land directly on a non-lobby room URL.

* fix: use semaphore to prevent TOCTOU race in session concurrency limit

The previous Mutex-based check in connect() released the lock before
spawning the agent thread, allowing concurrent callers to bypass the
max_concurrent cap. Replace with a tokio Semaphore whose permits are
held in SessionEntry for the session lifetime, making slot reservation

commit.message:
feat: add Show mode for card show / trade night hunting (#54)

* feat: add Show mode for card show / trade night hunting

New tab optimized for finding and acquiring missing cards at physical
card shows and trade nights. Mobile-first design with:

- Missing-cards-only view with prominent remaining count
- Search by name, set, or card number for quick lookups
- Set filter chips to match vendor binder organization
- Market price display with TCGPlayer links for price reference
- Large tap-friendly 'Got it!' button with celebration animation
  (green flash, scale, haptic feedback via navigator.vibrate)
- Session stats tracking cards found and value acquired today
- Sort by set, price (high/low), or name
- Condition selector (NM/LP/MP) for price comparison
- Collection complete celebration when all cards are found

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review feedback on show mode

- Wire previewCard dialog to thumbnail tap (was dead code)
- Add onClick handler for mobile card preview (CardPreview is hover-only)
- Use parseCardNumber for alphanumeric set number sorting (fixes NaN)
- Guard handleGotIt against double-tap race condition
- Add aria-label to clear-search button for accessibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address round 2 review feedback

- Toggle checked state immediately instead of deferring via setTimeout
  (prevents lost state on tab switch / unmount)
- Use ref for synchronous double-tap guard (state check can be
  bypassed by batched updates)
- Auto-clear stale set filter when last card in set is checked off
